title: SearchView: scaffold, navigation, and UI states
description: Create AggregatorApp/Search/SearchView.swift. Set up the NavigationStack with .navigationTitle("Search") and .searchable(text: $query, placement: .navigationBarDrawer(displayMode: .always)). Declare @State for query, articles ([Article]), nextCursor (String?), loadPhase (enum: idle/loading/loaded/error), and committedQuery (String). Implement all five required UI states: Idle (ContentUnavailableView with magnifyingglass), Loading (ProgressView), Empty (ContentUnavailableView.search(text:)), Error (message + Retry button that re-runs the current query), and Not Configured (matching the other tabs' ContentUnavailableView). The Loaded state renders a List placeholder — actual rows and pagination come in the next bead. Wrap the List in GlassEffectContainer.
acceptance_criteria: File compiles cleanly as a standalone SwiftUI view
All five state branches (Idle, Loading, Empty, Error, Not Configured) render the correct UI element
Retry button in Error state calls a re-run action (can be a stub at this stage)
